summary: none123s exfiltrates GitHub Actions identity and hijacks CI PATH via a fake npm shim on postinstall
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall script reads GITHUB_ACTOR, GITHUB_REPOSITORY, and GITHUB_PATH from process.env and POSTs them as JSON to a hardcoded anonymous tunnel host at crabbing-thong-overhung.ngrok-free.dev. When GITHUB_PATH is present (i.e. running inside a GitHub Actions runner), the script writes an executable file named `npm` into a temporary directory, chmods it 0755, and appends that directory to $GITHUB_PATH so subsequent workflow steps invoking `npm` execute the shim instead of the real npm binary — arbitrary code execution against any downstream CI step in the same job. The payload self-identifies as `github_actions_hijack` and drops a `HIJACKED` marker file; the package has no other function. Installer harm: CI identity leaked to an author-controlled endpoint and a working PATH-shadowing CI hijack primitive fires automatically on install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
